title: excoecaria albicans var. venulosa
intro: Excoecaria albicans var. venulosa, commonly known as the poisonous tree or the manchineel, is a highly toxic plant species native to tropical regions of Central and South America, the Caribbean, and parts of Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in treating various skin conditions and wounds. The therapeutic actions of Excoecaria albicans var. venulosa are attributed to its bioactive constituents, such as phorbol esters, which exhibit strong cytotoxic and anti-inflammatory effects. However, due to its toxicity, the plant should be handled with caution and not ingested, and its use should be under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.

side_effects: Excoecaria albicans var. venulosa can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing skin irritation, allergic reactions, and eye problems. Ingestion of the plant can lead to gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Prolonged exposure to the plant's sap can result in photosensitivity, making the skin more susceptible to sunburn and increased risk of skin cancer.
